CA1806.fullDescription: 已创建新对象，但未使用过该对象；或已调用创建并返回新字符串的方法，但未使用过该新字符串；或 COM 或 P/Invoke 方法返回从未使用过的 HRESULT 或错误代码。

CA1822.fullDescription: 不访问实例数据或调用实例方法的成员可标记为 static。将方法标记为 static 后，编译器将向这些成员发出非虚拟调用站点。这样可使对性能比较敏感的代码获得显著的性能提升。

CA1850.fullDescription: 相比创建并管理 HashAlgorithm 实例来调用“ComputeHash”，使用静态“HashData”方法更高效。

CA1854.shortDescription: 首选 “IDictionary.TryGetValue(TKey, out TValue)” 方法
CA1854.fullDescription: 首选 “TryGetValue” 调用，而不是通过 “ContainsKey” 检查保护的字典索引器访问。“ContainsKey” 和索引器都会查找位于底层的密钥，因此使用 “TryGetValue” 会删除额外的查找。

CA1862.shortDescription: 使用 "StringComparison" 方法重载来执行不区分大小写的字符串比较
CA1862.fullDescription: 避免调用“ToLower”、“ToUpper”、“ToLowerInvariant”和“ToUpperInvariant”来执行不区分大小写的字符串比较，因为它们会导致分配。相反，首选调用采用“StringComparison”枚举值的“Contains”、“IndexOf”和“StartsWith”的方法重载来执行不区分大小写的比较。切换到使用采用“StringComparison”的重载可能会导致行为发生细微变化，因此在应用建议后执行全面测试非常重要。此外，如果不需要区分区域性的比较，请考虑使用“StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase”。

CA1869.fullDescription: 避免为每个序列化操作创建新的“JsonSerializerOptions”实例。请改为缓存和重用实例。仅使用“JsonSerializerOptions”实例可能会显著降低应用程序的性能。

CA2101.shortDescription: 指定对 P/Invoke 字符串参数进行封送处理
CA2101.fullDescription: 平台调用成员允许部分受信任的调用方，具有字符串参数，且不显式封送字符串。这可能导致潜在的安全漏洞。

CS0108.shortDescription: 成员隐藏继承的成员；缺少关键字 new
CS0108.fullDescription: 使用与基类型中的变量相同的名称声明了变量。但是，未使用关键字 new。此警告通知应使用 new；变量如同在声明中使用了 new 一样进行声明。

CS0414.shortDescription: 字段已被赋值，但从未使用过它的值

CS0649.shortDescription: 从未对字段赋值，字段将一直保持其默认值

CS4014.shortDescription: 由于此调用不会等待，因此在调用完成前将继续执行当前方法
CS4014.fullDescription:
当前的方法调用返回一个 Task 或 Task<TResult> 的 async 方法，并且不会将 await 操作符应用到结果中。对 async 方法的调用将启动异步任务。但是，由于未应用 await 操作符，程序将继续运行而不会等待任务完成。在多数情况下，这种行为并不是你想要的。通常，调用方法的其他部分依赖调用结果，或者至少从包含此调用的方法中返回前需要完成此被调用的方法。

一个同样重要的问题是在调用的 async 方法中产生的异常将发生什么情况。在返回 Task 或 Task<TResult> 的方法中产生的异常存储在返回的任务中。如果你不等待任务完成或显式检查异常，则异常将丢失。如果你等待任务完成，则此异常将重新抛出。

最佳的做法是你应始终等待此调用完成。

仅当你确定不需要等待异步调用完成，并且调用的方法不会产生任何异常时，你可以考虑取消警告。为此，你可以通过将调用的任务结果分配给一个变量来取消警告。

CS8600.shortDescription: 将 null 字面量或可能为 null 的值转换为非 null 类型。

CS8602.shortDescription: 解引用可能出现空引用。

CS8603.shortDescription: 可能返回 null 引用。

CS8604.shortDescription: 引用类型参数可能为 null。

CS8618.shortDescription: 在退出构造函数时，不可为 null 的字段必须包含非 null 值。请考虑添加 "required" 修饰符或声明为可为 null。

CS8625.shortDescription: 无法将 null 字面量转换为非 null 的引用类型。

S1006.shortDescription: Method overrides should not change parameter defaults
S1006.fullDescription: Default arguments are determined by the static type of the object.

S101.shortDescription: Types should be named in PascalCase
S101.fullDescription: Shared naming conventions allow teams to collaborate efficiently.

S1066.fullDescription: Nested code - blocks of code inside blocks of code - is eventually necessary, but increases complexity. This is why keeping the code as flat as possible, by avoiding unnecessary nesting, is considered a good practice.

S1075.fullDescription: Hard-coding a URI makes it difficult to test a program for a variety of reasons:

S1117.shortDescription: Local variables should not shadow class fields or properties
S1117.fullDescription: Shadowing occurs when a local variable has the same name as a variable, field, or property in an outer scope.

S1135.fullDescription: Developers often use TODO tags to mark areas in the code where additional work or improvements are needed but are not implemented immediately. However, these TODO tags sometimes get overlooked or forgotten, leading to incomplete or unfinished code. This rule aims to identify and address unattended TODO tags to ensure a clean and maintainable codebase. This description explores why this is a problem and how it can be fixed to improve the overall code quality.

S1144.shortDescription: Unused private types or members should be removed
S1144.fullDescription: This rule raises an issue when a private/internal type or member is never referenced in the code.

S1172.shortDescription: Unused method parameters should be removed
S1172.fullDescription: A typical code smell known as unused function parameters refers to parameters declared in a function but not used anywhere within the function’s body. While this might seem harmless at first glance, it can lead to confusion and potential errors in your code. Disregarding the values passed to such parameters, the function’s behavior will be the same, but the programmer’s intention won’t be clearly expressed anymore. Therefore, removing function parameters that are not being utilized is considered best practice.

S1192.shortDescription: String literals should not be duplicated
S1192.fullDescription: Duplicated string literals make the process of refactoring complex and error-prone, as any change would need to be propagated on all occurrences.

S1244.shortDescription: Floating point numbers should not be tested for equality
S1244.fullDescription: Floating point numbers in C# (and in most other programming languages) are not precise. They are a binary approximation of the actual value. This means that even if two floating point numbers appear to be equal, they might not be due to the tiny differences in their binary representation.

S125.shortDescription: Sections of code should not be commented out
S125.fullDescription: Commented-out code distracts the focus from the actual executed code. It creates a noise that increases maintenance code. And because it is never executed, it quickly becomes out of date and invalid.

S1481.shortDescription: Unused local variables should be removed
S1481.fullDescription: An unused local variable is a variable that has been declared but is not used anywhere in the block of code where it is defined. It is dead code, contributing to unnecessary complexity and leading to confusion when reading the code. Therefore, it should be removed from your code to maintain clarity and efficiency.

S1871.shortDescription: Two branches in a conditional structure should not have exactly the same implementation
S1871.fullDescription: When the same code is duplicated in two or more separate branches of a conditional, it can make the code harder to understand, maintain, and can potentially introduce bugs if one instance of the code is changed but others are not.

S2325.shortDescription: Methods and properties that don't access instance data should be static
S2325.fullDescription: Methods and properties that don’t access instance data should be marked as static for the following reasons:

S2696.shortDescription: Instance members should not write to "static" fields
S2696.fullDescription: This rule raises an issue each time a static field is updated from a non-static method or property.

S3168.fullDescription: An async method with a void return type does not follow the task asynchronous programming (TAP) model since the return type should be Task or Task<TResult>

S3260.shortDescription: Non-derived "private" classes and records should be "sealed"
S3260.fullDescription: Classes and records with either private or file access modifiers aren’t visible outside of their assemblies or files, so if they’re not extended inside their scope, they should be made explicitly non-extensible with the addition of the sealed keyword.

S3267.shortDescription: Loops should be simplified with "LINQ" expressions
S3267.fullDescription: Using explicit loops for filtering, selecting, or aggregating elements can make code more verbose and harder to read. LINQ expressions provide a more concise and expressive way to perform these operations, improving code clarity and maintainability.

S3358.shortDescription: Ternary operators should not be nested
S3358.fullDescription: Nested ternaries are hard to read and can make the order of operations complex to understand.

S3400.fullDescription: There’s no point in forcing the overhead of a method call for a method that always returns the same constant value. Even worse, the fact that a method call must be made will likely mislead developers who call the method thinking that something more is done. Declare a constant instead.

S3459.fullDescription: Fields and auto-properties that are never assigned to hold the default values for their types. They are either pointless code or, more likely, mistakes.

S3604.shortDescription: Member initializer values should not be redundant
S3604.fullDescription: Fields, properties and events can be initialized either inline or in the constructor. Initializing them inline and in the constructor at the same time is redundant; the inline initialization will be overridden.

S3626.shortDescription: Jump statements should not be redundant
S3626.fullDescription: Jump statements, such as return, yield break, goto, and continue are used to change the normal flow of execution in a program. However, redundant jump statements can make code difficult to read and maintain.

S3776.shortDescription: Cognitive Complexity of methods should not be too high
S3776.fullDescription: This rule raises an issue when the code cognitive complexity of a function is above a certain threshold.

S4201.shortDescription: Null checks should not be combined with "is" operator checks
S4201.fullDescription: There’s no need to null test in conjunction with an is test. null is not an instance of anything, so a null check is redundant.

S4487.fullDescription: Private fields which are written but never read are a case of "dead store". Changing the value of such a field is useless and most probably indicates an error in the code.

S4790.shortDescription: Using weak hashing algorithms is security-sensitive
S4790.fullDescription: Cryptographic hash algorithms such as MD2, MD4, MD5, MD6, HAVAL-128, DSA (which uses SHA-1), RIPEMD, RIPEMD-128, RIPEMD-160and SHA-1 are no longer considered secure, because it is possible to have collisions (little computational effort is enough to find two or more different inputs that produce the same hash).

S5042.shortDescription: Expanding archive files without controlling resource consumption is security-sensitive
S5042.fullDescription: Successful Zip Bomb attacks occur when an application expands untrusted archive files without controlling the size of the expanded data, which can lead to denial of service. A Zip bomb is usually a malicious archive file of a few kilobytes of compressed data but turned into gigabytes of uncompressed data. To achieve this extreme compression ratio, attackers will compress irrelevant data (eg: a long string of repeated bytes).

S6444.shortDescription: Not specifying a timeout for regular expressions is security-sensitive
S6444.fullDescription: Not specifying a timeout for regular expressions can lead to a Denial-of-Service attack. Pass a timeout when using System.Text.RegularExpressions to process untrusted input because a malicious user might craft a value for which the evaluation lasts excessively long.

S6562.shortDescription: Always set the "DateTimeKind" when creating new "DateTime" instances
S6562.fullDescription: Not knowing the Kind of the DateTime object that an application is using can lead to misunderstandings when displaying or comparing them. Explicitly setting the Kind property helps the application to stay consistent, and its maintainers understand what kind of date is being managed. To achieve this, when instantiating a new DateTime object you should always use a constructor overload that allows you to define the Kind property.

S6580.shortDescription: Use a format provider when parsing date and time
S6580.fullDescription: When converting a string representation of a date and time to a DateTime object or any other temporal type with one of the available system parsing methods, you should always provide an IFormatProvider parameter.

S6966.fullDescription: In an async method, any blocking operations should be avoided.

S907.fullDescription: goto is an unstructured control flow statement. It makes code less readable and maintainable. Structured control flow statements such as if, for, while, continue or break should be used instead.

SYSLIB1054.shortDescription: 使用 “LibraryImportAttribute” 而不是 “DllImportAttribute” 在编译时生成 P/Invoke 封送代码
SYSLIB1054.fullDescription: 使用 “LibraryImportAttribute” 而不是 “DllImportAttribute” 在编译时生成 P/Invoke 封送代码
